zh.lifeSummary: 當Dorothy Bishop於1574年出生在威爾士布洛可爾時，她的父親Matthew Bishop40歲，母親Elizabeth Young36歲。她於1600年之前與John Meyrick在威爾士布洛可爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1650年6月25日在英國格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托享年76歲，埋葬在英國布里斯托。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dorothy Bishop于1574年出生在威尔士布洛可爾时，她的父亲Matthew Bishop40岁，母亲Elizabeth Young36岁。她于1600年之前与John Meyrick在威尔士布洛可爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1650年6月25日在英国格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托享年76岁，埋葬在英国布里斯托。

nameMeanings.name: Bishop
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English bissop, biscop, Old English bisc(e)op ‘bishop’, which comes via Latin from Greek episkopos ‘overseer’. The Greek word was adopted early in the Christian era as a title for an overseer of a local community of Christians, and has yielded cognates in every European language: French évêque, Italian vescovo, Spanish obispo, Russian yepiskop, German Bischof, etc. The word came to be applied as a surname for a variety of reasons, among them a supposed resemblance in bearing or appearance to a bishop, and selection as the ‘boy bishop’ on Saint Nicholas's Feast Day. In some instances the surname is from the rare Middle English (Old English) personal name Biscop ‘bishop’. As an Irish surname it is adopted for Mac Giolla Easpaig, meaning ‘servant of the bishop’ (see Gillespie ). In North America, this surname has absorbed, by assimilation and translation, at least some of continental European cognates, e.g. German Bischoff , Polish, Rusyn, Czech, and Slovak Biskup , Slovenian Škof (see Skoff ).

variants.name: Bischoff
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Bernhard, Hans, Klaus, Lorenz, Alois, Armin, Arno, Dieter, Fritz.

German: from Middle High German bischof, Middle Low German bischop ‘bishop’, probably applied as a nickname for someone with a pompous manner or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a bishop. See also Bishop .

variants.name: Bispo
meanings: Portuguese and Galician: nickname from bispo ‘bishop’ (see Bishop ).

variants.name: Gillespie
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Donegal): shortened Anglicized form of Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Easbuig, borrowed into Irish as Mac Giolla Easpaig, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of the bishop’.

variants.name: Episcopo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Orazio, Pasquale, Rocco.

Italian: from episcopo ‘bishop’ (from Latin episcopus (see Bishop ), probably a nickname for someone who had a pious manner or perhaps a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a bishop).

variants.name: Dean
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu), or a habitational name from any of several places in various parts of England named Dean or Dene from this word.

English: nickname or occupational name for the servant of a dean or nickname for someone thought to resemble a dean. A dean was an ecclesiastical official, the head of a chapter of canons or a church official with jurisdiction over a sub-division of an archdeaconry. Though no doubt some deans had illegitimate children, they were officially celibate, and in the main the surname is probably a nickname in origin, similar to Bishop , Prior , Priest , and Monk . The Middle English word deen, dien, dein, is a borrowing of Old French d(e)ien, doien from Latin decanus (originally a leader of ten men, from decem ‘ten’), and thus is a cognate of Deacon .
English: from the Middle English personal name Deyne (or Dene) a rhyming pet form of Reynald (see Reginald ).

nameMeanings.name: Dorothy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothee
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothie
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Dorothy Bishop was born in 1574, in Pembrokeshire, Wales, her father, Matthew Bishop, was 40 and her mother, Elizabeth Young, was 36. She married John Meyrick before 1600, in Pembrokeshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 25 June 1650, in Bristol, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 76, and was buried in Bristol, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Dorothy Bishopが1574年にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアで生まれた時，父Matthew Bishopは40歳，母Elizabeth Youngは36歳であった。1600年以前にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアでJohn Meyrickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1650年6月25日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブリストルで76歳で死去し，イギリスブリストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Dorothy Bishop(이)가 1574년 웨일스 p브로케실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Matthew Bishop(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Young(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1600년 이전 웨일스 p브로케실르에서 John Meyrick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1650년 6월 25일 영국 그루스스터실르 브리스톨에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 브리스톨에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dorothy Bishop родилась в 1574 г. в местности пемброкешир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Matthew Bishop было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Young, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Meyrick перед 1600 г. в местности пемброкешир, Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 25 июня 1650 г. в местности бристол, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 76 лет и была похоронена в местности бристол, Англия.
